pages.raw_text:
Years ago in Brown county,
South Dakota, man named Camp
bell bankrupted himself trying to
make crops grow with less rain
than had always been found neces-
sary for them. Others lost their
ALL too, and moved away cursing
the country. ......-..


Brown county and all its neigh
boring counties went down into the
slough of despond and foreclosures,
and great areas from Devils lake
on the north to Ell Paso on the
south lapsed itno the hands OF
mortgages, were wholesaled out in
big tracts to spectators, and have
now gone into prosperous farms
again.


Campbell has been the cause OF
this recovery more than any other
human factor. The other factor has
been some 10 years of plentiful
rainfall for the semi-arid and arid
west


Instead of giving ZIP, Campbell
devised a system of trapping and
saving in the earth the rainfall that
did come, and found that by proper
culture one inch of water will grow
as much grain as two inches will
grow under wet country methods.


But there are people who say,
with much reason, that even under
dry farming" methods there Is
danger of a great calamity to set
tiers all over this arid west when
the weather swings back to the dry
stage again, as it will. Even dry
farming" requires some water. And
when the arid plains go dry they
go dry. Otherwise they would not
be inhabited by prairie dogs and
grown up to cactus.


But they will always produce cac-
tus. Every year is a good cactus
year. And now come the grave and
reverenced young men of the agri-
cultural department and tell us that
cactus is probabl ythe best crop TIC
which any farm can be planted!
Not the spineless cactus of Bur
bank, but the old spiny, prickly
pear on which cattle are afraid to
step and which live stock must
have Harveyized lips to eat
You are Llano Estacado or
Colorado valley farmer. We will
say, on land too high for irrigation.
You plant cactus-plant 1t carefully
and till it. 1t won't be hurt by
weeds, for weeds won't grow there |;
but it will grow like the green bay
tree of scripture. The rabbits will
shun its thorns. The very goats
will go around it. But when you


want to feed it you take a plumb
er's torch and harvest it.


No, this isn't joke. You simply
take Slow torch and burn off the


spiits!f They are dry as matches
and SINCE off like the pin feathers
on a dressed chicken.


| When the cacti have been singed
the cattle, swine and sheep will
